comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: Plan-review: PASS (signed). Impl commit 29d9fa863. Triage of 19 advisories: (COH/E1/G6 — the important one) the ticket's literal compose 0.85*0.8=0.68 < 0.70 for a self-revealing hard-override finding defeats 'auto-high'; FIXED by flooring the override LAST (after the amplifier), mirroring impact_code's reversibility_floor — all three mechanisms (MAX/override/amplifier) preserved, AC2 intent met. (G1G2/T8 'verify prompt singular') BOTH plan_review_verifier.md + plan_review_verifier_agentic.md updated. (T1/T8 axis scoring) each axis has a clear none|low|medium|high definition in both the pydantic Field descriptions AND the prompts; hard-override + detection axes flagged. (E5/E6) test_impact_plan.py adds boundary tests + proving cmd; (AC4 'stranded 0.60-0.69 band clears 0.70') covered by a unit test (mean=0.6675 stranded, impact_plan>=0.70) — full-CORPUS replay is #3's calibration harness. (G6/E4/E3 'undecomposed from G5 child') the axis is emitted by the verifier; #4's veto ensures false 'flat' findings don't reach it. (T5e ADR) the per-gate impact_fn seam is documented in docstrings; #6's ADR will record the two-gate impact model. (back-compat) missing axis -> 0, older/absent verifier abstains; kernel 'verification' contract byte-identical (test asserts base model unchanged, plan model is a strict superset).

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: STATUS (handoff): IMPLEMENTED + COMMITTED as stack commit 29d9fa863. Plan-review PASS+signed; pre-commit lint+mypy PASSED; tests/unit/test_impact_plan.py (16) + updated test_review_kernel invariant green. Introduced the per-gate impact_fn seam on pass3_decide/pass3_over_findings (code-review byte-unchanged) + decide.impact_plan (MAX+override-floor-LAST+detection; fixed the 0.68<0.70 coherence bug) + verify.plan_review_verification_model. NOT CLOSED: awaiting Gerrit Verified. NOTE: later stack children (#6/#3) reference impact_fn from this commit — their gates must ground against the stack via REBAR_GATE_REF (see epic comment).

compiled_state.description:
## Why
The shared `impact = mean(severity attributes)` dilutes a genuinely high-severity plan finding below the
0.70 bar (a critical finding averaged with low axes lands ~0.60-0.69). The mean is the wrong aggregator.

## What
For the plan-review gate, replace the mean with severity-first MAX + hard override + detection amplifier.
- SEVERITY axes (each mapped to [0,1] by the verify pass): AC-unverifiable, DoD-uncertifiable, undecomposed
  (from the deterministic G5 signal), divergent-implementation, internal-conflict, vague-directive,
  irreversible-without-rationale.
- `impact_sev = MAX(axis contributions)` (no averaging).
- HARD OVERRIDE: if ANY of {AC-unverifiable, DoD-uncertifiable, undecomposed, divergent-implementation},
  `impact_sev = max(impact_sev, 0.85)`.
- DETECTION AMPLIFIER: `silent-vs-self-revealing` (silent build-the-wrong-thing -> x1.0, self-revealing
  wall -> x0.8); DoD-uncertifiable forces x1.0. Explicit compose: `impact = min(1.0, impact_sev * detection_mult)`.

## Implementation mechanics (grounded in the current code)
- VERIFY SCHEMA: the shared `verification_model()` in `verify.py` carries only the 5-field `SeverityAttrs`
  (prod_impact, debt_impact, blast_radius, likelihood, reversibility). Add a `plan_review_verification_model()`
  with an extended `PlanSeverityAttrs` adding the 7 axis fields above, EACH defaulting to `'none'`/absent so
  an older or absent verifier abstains (back-compat: a missing axis contributes 0, never inflates).
- PER-GATE DISPATCH (precise): `impact()` is called INSIDE the kernel's `pass3_decide()`, so it is NOT the
  consumer-injected-callable pattern `threshold_for` uses. Add `impact_fn: Callable[[dict], float] | None =
  None` to `pass3_decide()` defaulting to the existing `impact`; `pass3_over_findings` accepts and threads it;
  plan-review's wrapper passes `impact_fn=impact_plan`. The code-review gate later passes its own; the
  plan-review signed-verdict shape stays byte-compatible.

## Scope
- `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py` (`impact_fn` param + `impact_plan`), `verify.py`
  (`plan_review_verification_model` + `PlanSeverityAttrs`), the plan-review verify prompt (emit the 7 axes),
  and the orchestrator wrapper wiring. Depends on the deterministic G5 signal child. No code-review changes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `pass3_decide()` accepts an `impact_fn` (default = existing `impact`); plan-review threads `impact_plan`
      through `pass3_over_findings`; a test asserts the code-review path (no `impact_fn`) is byte-unchanged.
- [ ] `impact_plan = MAX(axes)` with the hard-override list and the detection amplifier (silent x1.0 /
      self-revealing x0.8; DoD-uncertifiable forces x1.0), per the explicit compose above.
- [ ] `plan_review_verification_model()` + `PlanSeverityAttrs` add the 7 axes with abstaining defaults;
      older/absent verifiers do not inflate impact.
- [ ] Offline replay: previously-stranded 0.60-0.69 band findings clear 0.70; verdicts stay stable on
      same-material replicate pairs.
compiled_state.title: Plan-review impact redesign: severity-first MAX + hard override + detection amplifier
